Plain-function handlers, a provider chain, and a context.Context parameter.
-- go.mod --
module app

go 1.26
-- main.go --
package main

func main() {
	if err := run(); err != nil {
		panic(err)
	}
}
-- store/store.go --
package store

import "context"

type Config struct{ DSN string }

//fabrik:provider
func NewConfig() Config { return Config{DSN: "mem"} }

type DB struct{ cfg Config }

//fabrik:provider
func Open(ctx context.Context, cfg Config) *DB { return &DB{cfg: cfg} }
-- web/web.go --
package web

import (
	"net/http"

	"app/store"
)

type API struct {
	DB *store.DB
}

//fabrik:http GET /api
func (a *API) Get(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {}

//fabrik:http POST /api
func (a *API) Post(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {}

//fabrik:http GET /health
func Health(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {}
-- want/main.gen.go --
// Code generated by fabrik. DO NOT EDIT.

package main

import (
	"app/store"
	"app/web"
	"context"
	"net/http"
	"os"

	"github.com/gofabrik/fabrik/router"
)

func run() error {
	// Init
	ctx := context.Background()

	// Providers
	r := router.New()
	storeConfig := store.NewConfig()
	storeDB := store.Open(ctx, storeConfig)
	webAPI := &web.API{
		DB: storeDB,
	}

	// Routes
	r.Method("GET", "/api", webAPI.Get)
	r.Method("POST", "/api", webAPI.Post)
	r.Method("GET", "/health", web.Health)

	// Serve
	addr := ":8080"
	if p := os.Getenv("PORT"); p != "" {
		addr = ":" + p
	}
	return http.ListenAndServe(addr, r)
}
